summary:
The selected answer is C because finasteride inhibits 5α-reductase leading to reduced dihydrotestosterone levels which consequently shrinks the prostate gland by decreasing cellular proliferation within the organ.
The effectiveness of finasteride has been substantiated across multiple studies highlighting its role as an effective treatment for benign prostatic hyperplasia related symptoms including enlarged prostate sizes. To summarize:

Finasteride effectively reduces prostate size via suppression of 5α-reductase activity lowering DHT concentrations; this process diminishes cell growth inside the prostate thereby shrinking overall glandular mass. Multiple research supports these findings demonstrating efficacy against BPH-related issues such as increased urinary frequency or urgency due primarily from larger than normal prostates caused often formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 1 summarized): The selected answer is C because finasteride inhibits 5α-reductase leading to reduced dihydrotestosterone levels which consequently shrinks the prostate gland by decreasing cellular proliferation within the organ.
The effectiveness of finasteride has been substantiated across multiple studies highlighting its role as an effective treatment for benign prostatic hyperplasia related symptoms including enlarged prostate sizes. To summarize:

Finasteride effectively reduces prostate size via suppression of 5α-reductase activity lowering DHT concentrations; this process diminishes cell growth inside the prostate thereby shrinking overall glandular mass. Multiple research supports these findings demonstrating efficacy against BPH-related issues such as increased urinary frequency or urgency due primarily from larger than normal prostates caused often
